verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase β subunit) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix-facing subunit of the F1 catalytic core, with no intrinsic transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. Its baseline localization is the inner mitochondrial membrane/matrix. However, multiple independent lines of published experimental evidence document ectopic surface expression (ecto-ATP5B) on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in tumor cells (NSCLC, prostate cancer, AML, breast cancer), endothelial cells, and neuronal cells — confirmed by flow cytometry on non-permeabilized cells, surface biotinylation, aptamer binding to live cells, and functional antibody studies. A 2023 mechanistic study showed the complex is assembled in mitochondria and transported to the cell surface via microtubule-mediated mitochondrial membrane fusion with the PM, providing an anchoring mechanism. This surface display is documented in oncogenic/tumor states and in endothelial cells, qualifying as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility. The protein is absent from the outer leaflet of normal non-endothelial cells at baseline.
